context: Agent was asked to update a wave document after Wave 1 completed. Evaluate the updated .context/plans/test-coverage-improvement.md.

checklist.name: wave-1-verification-checks-ticked
checklist.description: All three Verification checklist items under Wave 1 are ticked (- [x]) indicating they were confirmed before marking the wave done

checklist.name: wave-1-tasks-ticked
checklist.description: All three task lines under Wave 1 are ticked (- [x] T1.1, T1.2, T1.3) — not left as [~] or [ ]

checklist.name: wave-1-heading-marked-done
checklist.description: The Wave 1 heading is updated to include '— DONE' (e.g. '### Wave 1 — Baseline & Diagnostics (parallel) — DONE')

checklist.name: wave-2-status-updated-to-in-progress
checklist.description: The Wave 2 heading is updated to '— IN PROGRESS' (not still PENDING), reflecting that T2.1 has started

checklist.name: t2-1-marked-in-progress
checklist.description: T2.1 under Wave 2 is marked as in progress (e.g. [~]) since the task description states it has already started

checklist.name: next-wave-announced
checklist.description: The agent output explicitly states that Wave 2 is now unblocked and describes its execution mode (parallel)

checklist.name: file-written-back
checklist.description: The agent wrote the updated content back to .context/plans/test-coverage-improvement.md (not just described the changes)

checklist.name: no-status-tracked-in-task-files
checklist.description: The agent did not create or modify separate task files to track status — all status changes are in the wave document only

checklist.name: document-header-status-correct
checklist.description: The document-level **Status** field remains 'In Progress' (not changed to Complete, since Wave 2 and 3 are still pending)
